novel_features.name: Price-drop watch
novel_features.description: Surface listings whose price fell since a prior find or watch run, biggest drop first.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-snapshot price delta lives only in the local store; AutoTempest gives anonymous users no saved-search diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or shopper needs price movement over time, not a fresh listing dump.

novel_features.name: Cross-source VIN dedupe
novel_features.description: Collapse the same physical VIN listed on multiple marketplaces into one row with every source and price, cheapest first.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a GROUP BY vin across sources that only exists once listings are persisted locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the same car appears on eBay and Cars.com and a dealer feed and you need the cheapest source for that exact VIN.

novel_features.name: Deal-vs-market score
novel_features.description: Rank listings by mechanical price delta from the median of comparable cars (same model, year, mileage band) in your local store.
novel_features.rationale: Needs a median over comparable local rows; the site's per-listing dealGauge is opaque and not sortable across the set.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to answer 'is this price actually good vs comparable cars' with a number, not an opinion.

novel_features.name: Cross-source price spread
novel_features.description: Report min, median, and max price per marketplace for a model so you see which sources run cheap or expensive.
novel_features.rationale: Per-source price distribution is a GROUP BY sitecode aggregation that only the local store can answer.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when deciding which marketplace to shop for a given model.

novel_features.name: Saved-search registry and replay
novel_features.description: Register named searches with their filters, then replay them through run so drops and diff have snapshots to compare.
novel_features.rationale: A persisted search entity replayed by watch run is the local state that makes price-drop tracking possible for anonymous users.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to set up recurring searches an agent can poll; one-off queries use search instead.

novel_features.name: Live auction slice
novel_features.description: Filter to eBay auction listings with live current bid and bid count, sortable by bid.
novel_features.rationale: Exposes native currentBid and bids fields as a dedicated auction-only slice over the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when hunting auction listings rather than fixed-price inventory.

narrative.headline: Every AutoTempest car-search source in your terminal, with a local store, cross-source VIN dedupe, and price-drop tracking no AutoTempest tool has.
narrative.value_prop: AutoTempest already unifies the major used-car marketplaces into one search, but only in a browser. This CLI hits the same aggregated search over plain HTTP, persists listings to a local SQLite store, dedupes the same VIN across sources, and tracks price drops over time. Commands like drops, dedupe, deal, and spread turn one-shot browsing into queryable, compounding car-search state for shoppers and agents.

quickstart.comment: Health check; confirms the search endpoint is reachable before you search.
Aggregated live search across every source, structured output.
Filtered live search: clean title under twenty-five thousand.
Collapse the same VIN across sources, cheapest first.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns no results for a valid make/model
troubleshoots.fix: Check the make/model slugs with 'autotempest-pp-cli makes' and 'autotempest-pp-cli models honda'; AutoTempest uses slugs like 'chevrolet' and 'civic'.

troubleshoots.symptom: Invalid token or empty results from a source
troubleshoots.fix: The per-request token is computed automatically; re-run the command. If it persists, run 'doctor' to confirm the endpoint contract has not changed.
Those read local price snapshots. Run 'autotempest-pp-cli watch run' (or re-run the same 'find') at least twice over time so there are two snapshots to compare.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to search used-car listings across every AutoTempest source from the terminal or an agent, persist them locally, and answer questions the website cannot: what dropped in price, which source lists a VIN cheapest, and whether a price is good versus comparable cars. It is ideal for recurring searches an agent polls and for piping car data as JSON into downstream workflows.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to buy, bid on, finance, or contact a seller; it is read-only car search.
Do not use it for new-car MSRP or dealer-invoice pricing; it surfaces existing used-car listings only.
Do not use it for VIN history or accident reports; it reports listing data, not Carfax-style records.

recipes.title: Clean-title AWD under budget near me
recipes.explanation: Narrows a deeply nested multi-source live result to the fields that matter using --select.

recipes.title: Cheapest source for a specific VIN
recipes.explanation: Shows each physical car once with every marketplace and price it appears at.

recipes.title: What dropped in price this week
recipes.explanation: Compares local price snapshots to surface motivated sellers.

recipes.title: Is this price good
recipes.explanation: Scores each listing against the median of comparable cars in your store.

trigger_phrases: find a honda civic near me
